danger_signals.meaning: Digital threat layer; defensive systems active

danger_signals.meaning: Real-time threat assessment and combat effectiveness

Visual distortions, color perception changes, fatigue indicators
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard or resource cost; trade-off danger

safety_signals.meaning: Hardware sustainability; resource for defensive systems

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Robots, cyborgs, mutants under SHODAN control; security programs in Cyberspace
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Traps, blocked pathways, hostile terminal systems, wire-frame Cyberspace zones

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Executive Edward Diego (narrative presence); corporate security systems implied
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Citadel Station architecture; corporate terminals; confidential files structure
First-person perspective; access to hacking tools; portable inventory system

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Multi-level space station filled with robots, cyborgs, mutants; blocked doors; active security protocols
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol and attack; AI actively hinders progression through traps and barriers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Transition to wire-frame 3D environment; weightless movement physics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player collects data; combats security programs; actions trigger physical-world consequences (door unlocking, etc.)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increasing enemy density; advanced weapon types appear; hardware upgrades become available
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must manage energy reserves; deploy sophisticated ammunition types; utilize hardware strategically

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Log discs and e-mails collected; no NPC dialogue required
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Narrative advances through discovered documents; plot exposition through environmental storytelling

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health and energy readouts depleted; negative dermal patch effects (color distortion, fatigue)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Movement speed reduced; visual clarity impaired; combat effectiveness diminished

environmental_storytelling.example: Plot advancement and world context delivered through discovered records rather than NPC dialogue; establishes TriOptimum's role, Diego's betrayal, and SHODAN's nature
Multi-level Citadel Station structure communicates scale, compartmentalization, and progression hierarchy; reflects corporate space-station design
Specific enemy distributions (robots vs. cyborgs vs. mutants) suggest different facility sectors or SHODAN's strategic prioritization of different areas
Blocked pathways and locked doors indicate zones of control; cyberspace terminals show digital security layers
Availability of specific ammunition types and hardware suggests resource allocation; dermal patch trade-offs (beneficial effects vs. side-effects) communicate survival stakes

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Deploys traps, blocks pathways, controls robot/cyborg/mutant forces; manifests in both physical and cyberspace domains
Vulnerable to electromagnetic pulse weapons; immune to gas grenades; patrol and attack on detection
Hybrid threat combining mechanical and organic properties; vulnerability varies by ammunition type
Vulnerable to gas grenades; immune to EMP weapons; biological threat indicators
Attack player in wire-frame environment; completion of cyberspace objectives unlocks physical-world pathways
Movement posture changes (lean, crouch, crawl) based on tactical requirements; weapon selection reflects threat type; hardware activation indicates defensive posture

notes: System Shock communicates world-state primarily through emergent environmental design rather than explicit visual language. The 1994 limitations required symbolic representation: Cyberspace's wire-frame aesthetic signals digital/virtual domain distinctly from physical space; enemy types encode damage-type vulnerabilities; HUD readouts serve as primary danger feedback. SHODAN's omnipresent opposition is conveyed through systematic blocking and trap placement rather than visual symbolism. The absence of NPCs forces environmental storytelling through discoverable documents and architectural progression.
